title: Daily Briefing — May 27
headline: Tech is leading, defensive sectors lag, but flows say don't chase the rip.
body: Right, so the big picture: Markets are grinding higher, with SPY at $750.13 and QQQ at $729.04 — both well above their 20 MA (SPY 735.28, QQQ 701.79), showing trend resilience. Volatility is easing (VIX 17.01) and Fear & Greed sits at 61.4 — solid greed but not in dangerous territory yet.

Sector-wise, XLK (tech) is the lone leader: RS 121.8, momentum 103.0, with mega-cap names like AAPL ($310.65 vs SMA20 $293.28) and DELL ($305.68 vs SMA20 $243.85) stretching well above their moving averages. Meanwhile, Energy, Materials, Staples, Healthcare, and Utilities are all lagging and flagged as high risk. Financials (XLF $51.66 vs SMA20 $51.58) are improving, but still not leading — keep an eye on the canary.

Contrarian lens: Most momentum is concentrated in tech and a handful of names, classic narrow breadth. Retail is probably piling into hot tickers, so don't get FOMO — remember, if it's in the press, it's in the price. Commodities (CL=F $89.64, USO $131.50, both crashing well below SMA20) and crypto (BTC-USD $74,813.77 vs SMA20 $78,295.74) are weak — another signal that flows are rotating out of the 'riskier' assets. Don't break something if it's simple and good: risk-on, but patience.
callout_body:
SPY support: 20 MA ($735.28)
QQQ support: 20 MA ($701.79)
XLK resistance: $184.39
XLE support: SMA200 ($49.37)
Energy & commodity bounce zones: CL=F SMA200 ($71.80), USO SMA200 ($89.48)
XLF weekly 20 MA for financials health check
action_items: Stick to tech leaders above 20 MA — AAPL, DELL, NVDA (watch for daily close below MA for exit)
Monitor financials (XLF) for any breakdown — if it closes below SMA20 ($51.58), tighten exposure
closing_quote: Patience, react, don’t predict — less trades, more wins. If bonds freak out, everything breaks.
